summary: Data teams are stuck in a defensive posture, passively receiving messy data and producing dashboards no one looks at, which perpetuates their status as cost centers. To break this cycle, data teams must actively integrate with the business and tie their work to critical initiatives rather than just going through the motions.
key_points: Data teams play 'not to lose' by passively processing data without adding strategic value
There's no incentive for upstream data producers to make data useful for analytics
Data teams are seen as cost centers because they stay tucked away from the business
Playing to win means integrating with business and tying data to critical initiatives
Most practitioners prefer passive report delivery over active business involvement
money_quotes: Data's still a mess. Most data initiatives fail. Data teams are seen as a cost center and not getting the support they deserve. Same as it ever was.
In most companies, there's no incentive to add value. It's easier to pass the buck to the people downstream from you. You play defense and make the next person pay.
The data team is just going through the motions, receiving messy data, doing 'data stuff' like making dashboards and staying tucked away from the business.
It all comes down to stopping the buck-passing and becoming actively involved in the business.
The reality is most of you won't do this. It's hard. And I'll probably write the same thing in five years. Same as it ever was.
key_insight: Data teams remain cost centers because they play defense instead of offense—to change this, they must stop passively processing data and start actively driving business outcomes.
